title: --sudo option: 6-layer elevation architecture with pulse tracing (v5.14)
desc: How --sudo triggers elevation across six coordinated layers: (1) MCP Launcher gate, (2) Core startup handler, (3) eye --sudo force-launch (non-destructive), (4) Eye auto-launch --sudo inheritance, (5) Pre-spawn hot-swap piggyback in LaunchElevatedEye, (6) Launcher --sudo always bypasses stale Eye pipe + implicit stderr passthrough. Pulse ring-buffer + reason dump make UAC prompts traceable.
steps:
LAYER 1 — MCP Launcher pre-execution gate (McpProxy.cs stdin router)
Pre-detects '"--sudo"' in JSON-RPC requests; preemptively calls SpawnAdminCore if admin not running. Existing reactive UIPI path untouched.

LAYER 2 — Core startup --sudo handler (Program.cs:233+)
Strips --sudo, checks IsElevated.
Branches: eye command → force-launch (layer 3) / other → proxy route.
PulseStep markers at every decision point (Init/Line/Finish).

LAYER 3 — eye --sudo non-destructive force-launch
Replaced old destructive kill+runas path. Now just calls LaunchElevatedEye side-by-side:
  if (IsAvailable()) → 'already running', exit 0
  else if (LaunchElevatedEye) → 'launched', exit 0
  else → 'UAC cancelled', exit 1
Old user Eye preserved; new admin Eye cleans up abnormal Eye itself.

LAYER 4 — Eye auto-launch --sudo inheritance (AppBotEyeCommands.cs)
Guardian args append ' --sudo' when Program.IsElevated()==true.
Admin Core respawning Eye → new Eye also admin+--sudo marker.
User Core respawning Eye → regular user Eye.

LAYER 5 — Pre-spawn hot-swap piggyback (v5.14)
LaunchElevatedEye, before runas+UAC, checks for wkappbot-core.new.exe.
If pending: rename current exe → backup name, rename .new.exe → .exe.
Admin Eye starts from latest code.
Rename of running exe is permitted on Windows (delete/overwrite is not).
Logs: '[ELEVATION:HOT-SWAP] pending .new.exe detected — promoting before admin spawn'

LAYER 6 — Launcher-side --sudo bypass + stderr passthrough (v5.14.x hotfix)
Bug 3 (stale Eye bypass): Launcher adds isSudoRequest to Eye pipe delegation skip condition.
  Prevents stale in-memory user Eye from handling --sudo with old code.
Bug 4 (stderr visibility): showStderr now implicitly true when --sudo in args.
  Otherwise IocpPipeRelay buffers stderr (invisible for DETACHED_PROCESS Core).
Without Layer 6 fixes, PULSE tracing + [ELEVATION] reason/trail never reach user.

PULSE TRACING — step-by-step debug via stderr
Program.cs uses PulseStep.Init/Line/Finish throughout --sudo block.
Force mode (Line) always emits (bypass WKAPPBOT_PROFILE env).
Ring buffer (20 lines) captured in PulseStep.GetRecentTrail — used by UAC prompt.

UAC REASON + TRAIL DUMP (ElevatedEyeProxy.LaunchElevatedEye(reason))
Before UAC, prints to stderr:
  [ELEVATION] ▼ Requesting admin rights: <reason>
  [ELEVATION] ── recent step trail ──
  [ELEVATION]   <last 5 pulse lines>
  [ELEVATION] ──────────────────────
Sites provide contextual reasons:
  eye force-launch: 'eye --sudo force-launch (Eye may be abnormal; recovery)'
  proxy route: '--sudo proxy route: command <cmd>'
  Readiness UIPI: 'Readiness: UIPI-blocked target, proxy fallback'

grep PULSE' — step-by-step flow
  2. Trail before UAC tells you which step requested admin
  3. tasklist

grep wkappbot-core → expect 2+ cores (user+admin)
  4. Admin pipe: Test-Path '\.\pipe\wkappbot_elevated'
  5. Regression: bin/wkappbot.hq/experience/tests/sudo/test-mcp-sudo-gate.sh (T1-T7)

KNOWN OPEN ISSUE (v5.14.x)
Bug 1: admin Eye receives request via pipe but hangs inside its request handler.
  Symptom: ExecuteViaProxy returns -1 after 5s OperationCanceledException.
  Retry also times out → returns exit 1.
  Requires deeper dive into admin Eye request processing.
author: claude
